SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.expander

Class TurboExpanderCompressorMechanicalDesign

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.MechanicalDesign
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.expander.TurboExpanderCompressorMechanicalDesign
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class TurboExpanderCompressorMechanicalDesign extends MechanicalDesign
Mechanical design for a coupled turbo-expander-compressor unit per API 617.

Unlike the standalone ExpanderMechanicalDesign or CompressorMechanicalDesign, this class considers both ends of the shared shaft simultaneously:

  • Expander wheel sizing (radial inflow, axial, mixed-flow selection)
  • Compressor impeller staging and sizing
  • Shared-shaft diameter from combined torque
  • Coupled rotor-dynamics (critical speeds across full bearing span)
  • Independent casing design for each end (different pressures)
  • Bearing system (journal and thrust) for combined radial and axial loads
  • Seal system (potentially different types at each end)
  • Module weight and footprint

After calcDesign() sizes the hardware at design-point conditions, call evaluateDesignAtConditions(double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double) or evaluateDesignWithFluid(neqsim.thermo.system.SystemInterface, double, neqsim.thermo.system.SystemInterface, double) to check whether the fixed design can handle different operating points (turndown, changed compositions, different pressures).

References:

  • API 617 - Axial and Centrifugal Compressors and Expander-compressors
  • API 612 - Steam Turbines
  • ASME Section VIII Division 1 - Pressure Vessels
  • ISO 10439 - Petroleum, petrochemical and natural gas industries - Centrifugal compressors
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team
See Also:

      polytropicHead - total polytropic head [kJ/kg]

    • evaluateDesignAtConditions

      public DesignEvaluationResult evaluateDesignAtConditions(double expInletP, double expOutletP, double expInletT, double compInletP, double compDischargeP, double compInletT, double expMassFlow, double compMassFlow, double expMolarMass, double compMolarMass)
      Evaluate whether the fixed mechanical design can handle the given operating conditions.

      All pressures in bara, temperatures in deg C, flows in kg/hr, molar masses in kg/kmol.

      Parameters:
      expInletP - expander inlet pressure [bara]
      expOutletP - expander outlet pressure [bara]
      expInletT - expander inlet temperature [deg C]
      compInletP - compressor suction pressure [bara]
      compDischargeP - compressor discharge pressure [bara]
      compInletT - compressor suction temperature [deg C]
      expMassFlow - expander mass flow [kg/hr]
      compMassFlow - compressor mass flow [kg/hr]
      expMolarMass - expander gas molar mass [kg/kmol]
      compMolarMass - compressor gas molar mass [kg/kmol]
      Returns:
      evaluation result with margins and pass/fail

    • evaluateDesignWithFluid

      public DesignEvaluationResult evaluateDesignWithFluid(SystemInterface expanderFluid, double expOutletP, SystemInterface compressorFluid, double compDischargeP)
      Evaluate the design using full thermodynamic fluid objects for more accurate results.

      Performs isentropic flash calculations to determine actual enthalpy drops rather than relying on ideal-gas approximations.

      Parameters:
      expanderFluid - expander inlet fluid (will be cloned, not modified)
      expOutletP - expander outlet pressure [bara]
      compressorFluid - compressor inlet fluid (will be cloned, not modified)
      compDischargeP - compressor discharge pressure [bara]
      Returns:
      evaluation result with margins and pass/fail

    • getTemperatureDeratedStress

      private double getTemperatureDeratedStress(double temperatureC)
      Get allowable stress with temperature derating.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - design temperature [deg C]
      Returns:
      allowable stress [MPa]

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Export mechanical design data to JSON format.

      This method creates a MechanicalDesignResponse object and serializes it to JSON using Gson. The JSON includes equipment identification, weight breakdown, design conditions, dimensions, and materials information.

      Usage example: 

       
 MechanicalDesign mecDesign = separator.getMechanicalDesign();
 mecDesign.calcDesign();
 String json = mecDesign.toJson();
      Overrides:
      toJson in class MechanicalDesign
      Returns:
      JSON string representation of the mechanical design

      public void setHotGasRecycle(boolean hotRecycle)

    • setFromDatasheet

      public void setFromDatasheet(double expanderWheelDiameterMm, double compressorImpellerDiameterMm, double ratedSpeedRpm, double maxContSpeedRpm, double tripSpeedRpm, double firstCritRpm, double secondCritRpm, double shaftDiameterMm, double bearingSpanMm, double expCasingDesignP, double expCasingDesignT, double compCasingDesignP, double compCasingDesignT)
      Load all key mechanical design parameters from a vendor datasheet specification.

      This allows evaluating an existing TEX unit against new operating scenarios without needing to run calcDesign() first. Call this method with the manufacturer's rated values, then call evaluateDesignAtConditions(double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double) for each off-design case.

      Parameters:
      expanderWheelDiameterMm - expander wheel outer diameter [mm]
      compressorImpellerDiameterMm - compressor impeller diameter [mm]
      ratedSpeedRpm - rated operating speed [rpm]
      maxContSpeedRpm - maximum continuous speed [rpm]
      tripSpeedRpm - overspeed trip speed [rpm]
      firstCritRpm - first lateral critical speed [rpm]
      secondCritRpm - second lateral critical speed [rpm]
      shaftDiameterMm - shaft diameter [mm]
      bearingSpanMm - bearing span [mm]
      expCasingDesignP - expander casing design pressure [bara]
      expCasingDesignT - expander casing design temperature [deg C]
      compCasingDesignP - compressor casing design pressure [bara]
      compCasingDesignT - compressor casing design temperature [deg C]

    • setRatedConditions

      public void setRatedConditions(double expInletP, double expOutletP, double expInletT, double expOutletT, double compSuctionP, double compDischargeP, double compSuctionT, double compDischargeT)
      Set rated operating conditions from vendor performance guarantee.
      Parameters:
      expInletP - expander rated inlet pressure [bara]
      expOutletP - expander rated outlet pressure [bara]
      expInletT - expander rated inlet temperature [deg C]
      expOutletT - expander rated outlet temperature [deg C]
      compSuctionP - compressor rated suction pressure [bara]
      compDischargeP - compressor rated discharge pressure [bara]
      compSuctionT - compressor rated suction temperature [deg C]
      compDischargeT - compressor rated discharge temperature [deg C]

    • setRatedPerformance

      public void setRatedPerformance(double expanderPower, double compressorPower, double expanderEnthalpyDrop, double compPolytropicHead, double surgeFlow, double stonewallFlow)
      Set rated performance data from vendor datasheet.
      Parameters:
      expanderPower - rated expander power [kW]
      compressorPower - rated compressor power [kW]
      expanderEnthalpyDrop - isentropic enthalpy drop [kJ/kg]
      compPolytropicHead - compressor polytropic head [kJ/kg]
      surgeFlow - surge line flow [m3/hr actual]
      stonewallFlow - stonewall flow [m3/hr actual]

    • evaluateMultipleScenarios

      public List<DesignEvaluationResult> evaluateMultipleScenarios(List<String> scenarioNames, List<Map<String,Double>> scenarios)
      Evaluate the fixed design against multiple operating scenarios simultaneously.

      Each scenario is defined as a Map&lt;String, Double&gt; with the following keys (all mandatory):

      • expInletP — expander inlet pressure [bara]
      • expOutletP — expander outlet pressure [bara]
      • expInletT — expander inlet temperature [deg C]
      • compInletP — compressor suction pressure [bara]
      • compDischargeP — compressor discharge pressure [bara]
      • compInletT — compressor suction temperature [deg C]
      • expMassFlow — expander mass flow [kg/hr]
      • compMassFlow — compressor mass flow [kg/hr]
      • expMolarMass — expander gas molar mass [kg/kmol]
      • compMolarMass — compressor gas molar mass [kg/kmol]
      Parameters:
      scenarioNames - descriptive name for each scenario
      scenarios - list of scenario parameter maps
      Returns:
      list of evaluation results, one per scenario

    • getScenarioValue

      private double getScenarioValue(Map<String,Double> scenario, String key)
      Get a value from a scenario map with a default of 0.0.
      Parameters:
      scenario - the scenario parameter map
      key - the parameter key
      Returns:
      the value, or 0.0 if not present

    • evaluationReportToJson

      public String evaluationReportToJson(List<String> scenarioNames, List<Map<String,Double>> scenarios)
      Generate a comprehensive evaluation report comparing the design against multiple scenarios.
      Parameters:
      scenarioNames - names for each scenario
      scenarios - list of scenario parameter maps
      Returns:
      JSON string with full evaluation report
